---
pagetitle: KDE Analysis | Capstone
title: TKDE Analysis using ks package
---

![](../../Images/3.png){width="500"}

# Getting Started

The code chunk below uses `p_load()` function of pacman package to check if the required packages have been installed on the computer. If they are, the packages will be launched.

The packages used are:

-   sf package is used for importing, managing, and processing geospatial data.
-   tidyverse package for aspatial data wrangling.
-   ks package to perform multivariate kernel density estimation (KDE).

```{r}
pacman::p_load(sf, tidyverse, ks, lubridate, rgl)
```

In the code chunk below, the [cleaned data files](DataPrep.qmd) are loaded into R.

```{r}
#| eval: false
# Load base map
mpsz = st_read(dsn = "../data/geospatial", 
               layer = "MPSZ-2019") %>%
  st_transform(crs = 3414) %>%
  summarize()

# Load rds file
pickup_loc <- read_rds("../data/rds/pickup_loc.rds") %>%
  mutate(pingtimestamp = as.integer(pingtimestamp)) %>%
  select(-trj_id)
dropoff_loc <- read_rds("../data/rds/dropoff_loc.rds") %>%
  mutate(pingtimestamp = as.integer(pingtimestamp)) %>%
  select(-trj_id)
pickup_sf <- st_as_sf(read_rds("../data/rds/pickup_sf.rds"))
dropoff_sf <- st_as_sf(read_rds("../data/rds/dropoff_sf.rds"))
```
# Without normalization

```{r}
#| eval: false
kde_ks_original <- kde(pickup_loc, H = Hlscv(pickup_loc))

summary(kde_ks_original$estimate)
```

# With normalization

```{r}
#| eval: false
# Normalize the data to the range [0, 1]
normalize <- function(x) {
  return ((x - min(x)) / (max(x) - min(x)))
}

pickup_norm <- as.data.frame(lapply(pickup_loc, normalize))

# Store the original ranges for scaling back
range_x <- range(pickup_loc$pickup_lng)
range_y <- range(pickup_loc$pickup_lng)
range_time <- range(pickup_loc$pingtimestamp)

kde_ks <- kde(pickup_norm, H = Hlscv(pickup_norm))

plot(kde_ks)
```
